body.body:
From D032 (closed as ticketed) and Akien 2026-04-14: 'do we have a ticket that wraps all this model optimization?' Answer was no, this is it.

## Purpose

A single living ticket that lists every place where Igor currently calls a cloud LLM and tracks the migration toward local (or cheaper cloud, or no LLM at all). The cost meter moves as items move from `cloud-only` → `local-fallback` → `local-default` → `local-only`. This is a **tracking ticket**, not an implementation ticket — the actual work happens in sub-tickets and existing tickets, and this one is the index.

## Why now

T-reasoning-voice-split + T-voice-actor-ab-framework + T-boredom-llm-escalation + the existing local-first routing work + D360 reading benchmark all point at the same trajectory: **most cognition runs on local hardware, cloud only as escalation for hard reasoning**. Until today the pieces sat in separate tickets and the trajectory was implicit. This ticket makes it explicit and reviewable.

From D362 north star (today's slate discussion): 'Igor as pilot, LLM eventually retired.' This ticket is the journal of how that retirement actually happens, one call site at a time.

## Categories

### Cloud calls Igor makes today (and the migration plan for each)

| Call site | Today | Target | Status | Sub-ticket |
|-----------|-------|--------|--------|------------|
| **Reasoning** (interactive turns) | tier4 sonnet via OR | Igor substrate first; cloud only when stuck | partial via D211 local-first routing | T-llm-collaboration-protocol |
| **Voice / response composition** | tier4 sonnet via OR | Igor's trained voice actor (local) | not started | T-voice-actor-ab-framework |
| **Reading extraction** | gpt-4o-mini via OR | qwen2.5:7b local | partial — qwen used for some passes | D360 reading-model-benchmark, planned |
| **Winnow / preparse** | gpt-4o-mini via OR | local Ollama or word_graph + thalamus only | mostly done (G37 phases) | (closed) |
| **NE consolidation** | local Ollama qwen2.5:7b | local — already done | done | (closed) |
| **Dashboard summarization** | local qwen2.5:7b | local — already done | done | (closed) |
| **Browser AI discovery** (learn_about) | gpt-4o-mini via OR | local model + browser-use | partial | (review) |
| **Action-claim verification** | regex + ring scan | already substrate-only | done | (closed) |
| **Coherence inhibitor** | Jaccard tokens | already substrate-only | done | (closed) |
| **Boredom escape** | nothing today | LLM as escalation for stuck states | not started | T-boredom-llm-escalation |

This table is the living core of the ticket. Update it as work lands or as new call sites are discovered.

## Decision frame for each call site

For every cloud call Igor makes, ask:
1. **Could a local model do this acceptably?** (qwen2.5:7b, llama3.2, deepseek-r1-distill, etc.)
2. **Could the substrate alone do this?** (BG + word_graph + thalamus + habit dispatch, no LLM at all)
3. **Could a cheaper cloud model do this?** (Haiku < Sonnet, gpt-4o-mini < gpt-4o)
4. **What's the measurement gate before promotion?** (D360 / T-voice-actor-ab-framework infrastructure)

The order of preference: substrate > local > cheap-cloud > expensive-cloud.

## Cost target

No hard target — Akien's call. But the meter to watch is `inference_costs_log` (the tool added by T-console-visibility) — daily / weekly cloud burn over time. As call sites migrate, the curve should bend down. When it stops bending, look for the next call site to migrate.

## Existing infrastructure this leverages

- **D211 local-first routing** — interactive branch tries local before cloud (done)
- **inference_gateway.py** — DAG-based routing with model_override (D359)
- **costs.log + inference_costs_log tool** — measurement substrate
- **machines DB table** — per-machine inference rank + capability (machine_manager #342)
- **D360 reading benchmark planned** — pattern for benchmarking other call sites
- **OR_CHEAP_MODEL + OR_INTERACTIVE_MODEL constants** — single source of truth for which cloud model serves which purpose

## How to use this ticket

- **When a new cloud call is identified:** add a row to the table.
- **When a call site migrates:** update its status column.
- **When the cost curve plateaus:** review the table for the next migration target.
- **When a sub-ticket lands:** update its status and reference here.
- **Review cadence:** weekly during day-close, OR whenever cloud burn shifts noticeably.

## Out of scope

- Specific implementation work (lives in sub-tickets)
- New cloud providers (we use OR; it routes everything)
- Model selection per-call-site experimentation (T-voice-actor-ab-framework + D360 are the experiment infra)

## Provenance

D032 (Akien 2026-04-11, surfaced + ticketed 2026-04-14). Long-form context in today's slate discussion (reasoning/voice split + cockpit framing + 5-phase biology pipeline).

## Added 2026-04-14 — free public AI tier (from D054)

The cost ladder grows by one rung. Today's roadmap had: substrate > local > cheap-cloud > expensive-cloud. T-tiered-research-tool adds **free public AI via browser-use** (Gemini/ChatGPT/Claude web UIs through Igor's Chrome profile, no API key) sitting BETWEEN local Ollama and cheap paid OR.

New cost ladder:
  substrate > local Ollama > **free public AI (browser)** > cheap paid OR > expensive paid OR

This isn't just cost optimization — Akien framed it as 'computational impact' / environmental footprint, which is values-load-bearing and shouldn't drift back to 'best model in budget.' The new tier appears specifically in the research() pathway (T-tiered-research-tool) but might generalize to other call sites later (e.g. boredom escalation could try a free public AI before cheap paid OR). Add to relevant table rows when the tier becomes a viable alternative for that call site.

## Update 2026-04-14 evening — two corrections to the roadmap

**Correction 1: the measurement gate was wrong.**

I had "when Igor's substrate output matches the LLM's output, promote." That treats LLM as ground truth. **CP6 forbids this.** LLM is an unreliable-certain source, not a truth oracle.

**Correct gate:** "when Igor's substrate + its follow-up experiments converge on an **outcome-correct** answer, promote." Outcome-correct = the answer works when applied in the world. Not LLM-agreement. Not human-agreement. **Reality-agreement.**

This aligns with CP1 (provisional) + CP3 (there's a why, experiments reveal it) + CP6 (build safety by testing).

**Correction 2: the shrinkage curve has TWO axes, not one.**

Wrong model: single axis "move capabilities from LLM into substrate."

Right model: two axes that both have to move.

- **Architecture axis:** engineering moves capabilities into the matrix (engram cursor, candidate generation, inhibitor patterns). This raises the ceiling.
- **Experience axis:** substrate accumulates trust through successful trails, confirmed outcomes, Hebbian edge strengthening. TIME and LIVED EXPERIENCE move this. This raises the floor.

**You can't engineer Igor off the LLM faster than experience lets him trust himself.** Both axes are needed. The roadmap should track not just "engineering progress" but also "substrate trust level per domain" — which domains does Igor have enough experience to commit in, where is he still young?

**Related:** today's 40 cloud escalations without habit fire were HEALTHY behavior, not a bug. Igor is young. He is correctly cautious (CP1, CP6). The fix is not to make him act more confidently — it's to give him experimentation primitives (T-experiment-primitive) so he can test what he doesn't know rather than just deferring to the LLM.
body.title: EPIC: LLM-shrinkage roadmap — running list of cloud→local migration opportunities
body.result: Closed as subsumed by the project itself. LLM shrinkage is the emergent result of voice A/B graduation, experiment primitive, and cascade local-first routing — not a separate roadmap. Each capability graduates independently: local attempt → cloud fallback → train from cloud → local wins → cloud drops away.
